behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-to-text model behavior (speaker diarization/label retention). A stubbed transcript would hide mislabeling or dropped speaker tags. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets STT output preservation itself, not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript chunks include stable speaker labels that can be mapped to attendee identities in downstream summary input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local parsing/transformation logic. The failure can be reproduced with deterministic transcript fixtures without real network, OS, or model output. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about extraction logic itself, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript snippets containing explicit assignments and assert structured output includes normalized assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality (coverage/completeness of action-item generation and attribution). Deterministic mocks would always pass and miss omissions. Rejected Workflow because behavior text requires output property of the live summary, not explicitly multiple model decision stages as the test target.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known assigned attendees, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Given already identified decisions, this is deterministic routing/mapping logic at a local code boundary. No real infra or model judgment is required. Rejected Integration because behavior text says 'routes identified decisions' (post-identification mapping), which can fail inside one module.
behaviors.test_description: Call the routing function with fixtures containing identified decision objects and assert they are emitted under `decisions` schema keys and excluded from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests model judgment/content placement: the LLM must recognize key decisions and place them in the correct section. Stubs hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on one live-summary decision-placement outcome, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit real transcripts with known key decisions to the real summarizer and assert those decisions appear in the `decisions` section, with checks that equivalent content is not only present in free narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple model calls in sequence (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge), with correctness depending on cross-step handoff and final coherence. Single-step tests miss sequencing and merge failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through end-to-end segmented processing, verify ordered segment execution, merged structured output integrity, and coherence across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans full pipeline with real recording and multiple AI stages (transcription then summarization) plus delivery. Real failures emerge from chained agentic steps and infrastructure interaction; lower levels cannot validate end-to-end autonomy.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting file: upload, real STT call, real LLM summarization, and output delivery; assert completion status, valid structured schema, and zero manual recovery steps.
